content.raw:
Current work, living systems, and useful experiments

A portfolio of AI culture, community infrastructure, keynote systems, and long-haul visual storytelling.

I build things that help people meet the future without losing the plot: public AI communities, practical training, talks that become reusable portals, and story systems that make technology feel human again.

Book a keynote Work with me
AI + cultureKeynotes, workshops, and field notes.
CommunityBC+AI, meetups, associations, and convening.
MediaPhotography, documentary, podcast, and broadcast.
Featured workStart with the live initiatives. Creative AI Human LabFilms, albums, portals, and reusable resources. Archive highlightsThe photo-heavy backstory. Start somethingBring me into the room.

Featured work

The center of gravity right now: a province-wide AI ecosystem, a brand-new responsible-AI certification, and hands-on training that helps teams build real confidence without flattening the weird human parts.

Ecosystem

BC + AI Ecosystem

A province-wide community and industry association for responsible, inclusive, place-rooted AI in British Columbia.

Role: Executive Director Year: 2026 Community: Vancouver AI and BC + AI Outcome: regional AI convening
Certification

Responsible AI Professional

Just launched. A four-week certification that closes the gap between how fast we deploy AI and how slowly we get responsible about it — built with Martin Lopatka and Sarah Downey. Every grad ships their own ethics assistant.

Role: Co-builder Year: 2026 Artifact: certification cohort Outcome: ethics assistant

Enroll in the cohort

Speaking

AI Keynotes

Talks and workshops on AI mindset, creative rebellion, human agency, archives, culture, and leadership after the point of no return.

Role: Keynote speaker Year: 2026 Community: creative workers Artifact: speaking page

See speaking topics

Indigenous tech

Indigenomics.ai

CTO work supporting Indigenous economic sovereignty, data governance, and tools grounded in the Indigenomics framework.

Role: CTO Year: 2026 Community: Indigenous economy Outcome: sovereignty tooling
Training

The Upgrade AI

Training, workshops, and applied AI sessions for creative professionals, organizations, community builders, and leadership teams.

Role: Co-founder Year: 2026 Community: creative teams Outcome: applied AI training

Explore services

Creative AI Human Lab

This is the reciprocal network for the creative AI experiments: films, albums, keynote portals, and process notes that keep the human parts visible. The throughline is consent, taste, community, and enough weirdness to keep the work alive.

Network hub

KrisKrug.co Work

The home base for the archive, field notes, services, speaking proof, and connective tissue across the creative AI network.

Role: Hub Year: 2026 Artifact: work page Outcome: network map
AI film + microsite

Skywhale Airways

I AM NOMAD is a 53-second AI Film Club short and hand-built site: a psychedelic airport for time travelers, made with Suzy Easton.

Role: Co-creator Year: 2026 Artifact: film + WebGL portal Outcome: festival materials
Creative rebellion

Punk Rock AI

A CreativeMornings Vancouver keynote rebuilt as a maker-first portal about agency, taste, and refusing to become a content machine.

Concept album

Too Weird to Die

A Suno-first Both Hands Full album about consent, defiance, survival, and refusing to sand the contradiction flat.

Community album

Ethos Lab Block Party

Eleven songs made from real conversations in one afternoon, with consent, first-name care, and the person as the hero.

Read the making-of

Protest design

Vancouver Made / MADE ON

A World Cup protest kit collection that took double silver at the Vancouver Made hackathon. Everyone else made a souvenir, we made the receipt, with every claim cited on the hem.

Read the making-of

How the work shows up

Same operating system, different surfaces: stages, training rooms, associations, media systems, and fieldwork.

AI education and adoption

Workshops, keynotes, demos, and custom sessions that help people build confidence without flattening the ethical and creative questions.

Vancouver AI and community infrastructure

Convening, association-building, sponsorship, event design, and systems that help networks become durable instead of merely loud.

Role: ConvenorYear: 2024-2026Community: Vancouver AIOutcome: durable network

Photography and media

Decades of documentary, conference, portrait, festival, and movement storytelling across technology, culture, and social change.

Role: PhotographerYear: 2005-2026Artifact: visual archiveOutcome: cultural documentation

Social impact and culture

Work spanning Indigenous-led community projects, youth media, climate gatherings, UN convenings, documentary fieldwork, and civic technology.

Archive highlights

The back catalog matters. It shows the pattern: cameras, communities, weird rooms, culture, civic technology, and enough miles on the road to know when an idea is real.

UN + youth media

UN Global Youth Summit on HIV, Bamako

Keynote and youth activist media mentoring work with UNAIDS in Mali, bringing online organizing and movement storytelling into a global room.

Climate + media

COP15 and climate storytelling

Hosted conversations and produced media around open culture, climate action, and digital community at major international gatherings.

Backstage culture

WE Day, conferences, and impossible rooms

Photography and video work in rooms where politics, celebrity, activism, and youth culture all collided.

Tech culture

Founders, networks, and the social web

Years documenting and convening the early social internet, startup communities, conferences, and the people who shaped the platforms.

Music + culture

Festivals, artists, and pop-cultural fieldwork

From Grammys corridors to festival fields, the work has always followed the living edge of culture.

Ideas festivals

PopTech, SXSW, TEDx, and idea circuits

Speaking, hosting, photographing, and learning inside the strange, generative spaces where culture and technology cross-pollinate.

More backstory Speaking archive Writing Start a project

Need a speaker, strategist, trainer, or weirdly useful AI collaborator?

Start with the lane that fits: keynotes for rooms that need energy and clarity, services for teams that need applied help, or contact if the project is odd and alive and probably worth a conversation.

Speaking Services Contact
